statement.headerDoc:
STEP Legendre — finite formalization of Legendre's conjecture for n ∈ [1, 100]
  =============================================================================

  Legendre's conjecture (1808, OPEN):
    ∀ n ≥ 1, there exists a prime p with n² < p < (n+1)².

  This file provides explicit prime witnesses for each n ∈ [1, 100] and
  proves via `native_decide` that each witness is prime and lies in the
  required open interval.

  Rei-AIOS cross-connection: witness(24) = 577, which is exactly the
  prime factor in peak 9232 = 2⁴·577 from Paper 118 Closure 5 (n=911
  universal on-ramp). This is an u

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Zero-sorry verified by lake env lean
